reasoning.champion: Arizona (seed 1, rating 89.8) won the championship over Houston (seed 2, rating 82.6). Score differential: +0.0814. Bracket total upsets: 12.

pool_recommendation.explanation: Maximum differentiation. Targets the highest-leverage champion the public is under-picking. Maximizes expected payout in crowded pools where owning an unpopular champion is the only path to a big win.
pool_recommendation.strategy_note: Large pool: the consensus champion is nearly worthless EV. Picking the same team as 150 other entries means you split the pot even if they win. Own your champion pick with maximum leverage — high win prob, low public share.
pool_recommendation.primary_reason: Illinois: 8.6% title probability, 2.6% public pick share (3.23× value, 31.4% Final Four probability). Estimated ~8 other entries in your pool will pick this team — but at 3.2× value, this is the highest-leverage champion available.

deterministic_recommendation.label: Deterministic
deterministic_recommendation.archetype: Pure model output, no pool adjustment
deterministic_recommendation.description: The model's single best prediction of the most likely tournament outcome. No pool-size differentiation applied. Use this as your baseline before layering in strategy.

safe_recommendation.label: Safe
safe_recommendation.description: Low-variance champion pick. Maximizes title probability. Best for shallow pools where being right matters more than differentiation — consensus picks pay well when the field is small.
safe_recommendation.primary_reason: Michigan has the highest title probability in the field (24.3%), 61.6% Final Four probability. In a shallow pool, picking the most likely winner maximises your chance of taking the pot outright.

value_recommendation.label: Value
value_recommendation.description: Balanced leverage. Targets a champion with positive value score (win prob > pick share) and seed ≤ 3. The sweet spot of meaningful win probability plus differentiation from the field.
value_recommendation.primary_reason: Illinois carries a 8.6% title probability against only 2.6% public pick share — 3.2× value, 31.4% Final Four probability. In a 60-person pool this is the sweet spot: meaningful win probability AND differentiation from the field.

contrarian_recommendation.label: Contrarian
contrarian_recommendation.description: Maximum differentiation. Targets the highest-leverage champion the public is under-picking. Maximizes expected payout in crowded pools where owning an unpopular champion is the only path to a big win.
contrarian_recommendation.primary_reason: Illinois: 8.6% title probability, 2.6% public pick share (3.23× value, 31.4% Final Four probability). Estimated ~13 other entries in your pool will pick this team — but at 3.2× value, this is the highest-leverage champion available.
